contest.title: 2025-2026 ICPC Asia Taichung Regional Contest (Unrated, Online Mirror, ICPC Rules, Teams Preferred)

problem.title: Circles Are Far from Each Other

sources.notes: Circle nesting/layout optimization with collinear centers.
Writeup binary-searches the answer and greedily maintains concentric-circle groups using deques.
Chinese statement mirror, useful for checking the geometric constraints.

annotation.summary: Place sorted-radius circles with collinear centers and nesting constraints to maximize the minimum distance between different circles.
annotation.constraints: n up to 1e5 and l up to 200; the answer is rational/real-like, so feasibility is tested by binary search.
annotation.core_idea: For a guessed minimum gap D, feasible layouts have the structure of several disjoint concentric-circle groups. Processing radii from small to large, groups whose outer radius is at most r_i-D are interchangeable for future containment. Greedily match mandatory contained circles first and track available/unavailable groups with deques; then verify the total collinear diameter budget.
annotation.complexity: O(n log precision) or O(n log n) depending on rational handling.

solution_variants.name: binary-search-greedy-groups
solution_variants.summary: Check a candidate gap by greedily assigning circles into concentric groups.

tags.evidence: The maximum feasible circle gap is found by binary searching the answer.
The feasibility check greedily matches available concentric groups when processing radii.
The solution relies on the geometry of nested collinear circles and distance between circumferences.
Feasibility is monotone in the guessed minimum gap.
